immutable_core.semantic_definition: A one-row-per-hospital-admission encounter baseline snapshot retaining one selected cleaned body-weight event in kilograms inside the approved admission-start baseline window, rather than a raw weight event stream, a patient-level stable weight, an ICU-start baseline, or a discharge/follow-up weight.
immutable_core.semantic_grain: one row per anchor-qualified summary target
immutable_core.same_name_cross_database_rule: Any database asset published as std_weight_admission_baseline must preserve admission-baseline encounter-start meaning, kilogram representation, and explicit anchor/window/selection semantics. It must not silently widen to generic patient-level weight or shift to ICU-start, discharge, or arbitrary nearest-weight semantics.

summary_contract.window_start_rule: start at the hospital admission timestamp
summary_contract.window_end_rule: end at the earlier of hospital discharge or hospital admission timestamp plus 1440 minutes
summary_contract.qualifying_row_rule: a qualifying source row is a cleaned weight event with a direct hospital-admission identifier, a non-null measurement timestamp, and a measurement timestamp inside the approved admission-baseline window
summary_contract.selection_rule: select the qualifying event with the smallest absolute time distance to the hospital admission timestamp
summary_contract.tie_break_rule: if multiple qualifying events are tied on time distance, prefer admission_weight_kg, then admission_weight_lbs converted to kg, then daily_weight_kg; if still tied, prefer earlier measurement time and then stable source_measurement_id order
summary_contract.no_source_row_action: do not retain a target encounter row when no qualifying baseline event exists under the approved source and window rules
summary_contract.partial_window_action: clip the baseline window at hospital discharge when discharge occurs before 24 hours after admission

canonical_representation.range_role_note: The hard range matches the current approved MIMIC retained-cleaning envelope. Values outside the plausible band require review but are not automatically impossible in critically ill adult records.

external_alignment.alignment_policy: Use external terminologies as semantic anchors when reliable, but do not make them the backbone of the standard-variable system.
external_alignment.public_anchor_status: broader body-weight observation anchors exist, but the admission-baseline encounter snapshot semantics are governed by this internal anchor/window contract
external_alignment.note: The external body-weight concept is a useful semantic anchor, but it does not by itself encode the admission-baseline window, target encounter grain, or no-source-row behavior.

new_variable_trigger_examples: changing std_weight_admission_baseline from admission-baseline encounter weight to generic subject-level body weight
changing the anchor from hospital admission start to ICU admission start or discharge time
version_bump_trigger_examples: adding a new reviewed database mapping while preserving the same admission-baseline meaning
tightening the source priority list or validation range after review without changing the baseline window semantics

allowed_database_specific_variation: raw source tables and source codes used to find local weight measurements
unit conversion route before canonical kilogram output
local structural keys used as provenance fields
availability of exact measured rows versus approved proxy rows, when explicitly reviewed
disallowed_same_name_variation: publishing a patient-level stable or historical weight as std_weight_admission_baseline
publishing an ICU-start baseline, discharge weight, first-day average, or arbitrary nearest weight as std_weight_admission_baseline without creating or approving a distinct variable contract

execution_governance.formal_output_rule: Formal std_weight_admission_baseline outputs should go through execution.py reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec. Execute-mode runtime evidence should record both spec hashes and formal output-file signatures.
execution_governance.future_non_bypassable_rule: Formal std_weight_admission_baseline outputs should be generated only through the governed execution.py path reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec.

not_yet_approved_database_notes: AmsterdamUMCdb-1.0.2 has a local candidate asset, but it remains built_pending_user_review and is not included in this public approved mapping set.
